current_entries.en_definition: (internet) To the home page.

examples.english: Why don't we go home?
examples.chinese: 我们何不回家呢？

current_entries.en_definition: A place of refuge, rest or care; an asylum.
One’s own dwelling place; the house or structure in which one lives; especially the house in which one lives with one's family; the habitual abode of one’s family.
The place (residence, settlement, country, etc.), where a person was born or raised; childhood or parental home; home of one’s parents or guardian.
(computers) Clipping of home directory.
One’s native land; the place or country in which one dwells; the place where one’s ancestors dwell or dwelt.
The locality where a thing is usually found, or was first found, or where it is naturally abundant; habitat; seat.
A land area free of woodland, cities, and towns; an area of open country.

examples.english: What's your major field?
examples.chinese: 你专攻什么领域？

current_entries.en_definition: A wide, open space that is used to grow crops or to hold farm animals, usually enclosed by a fence, hedge or other barrier.
A place where a battle is fought; a battlefield.
(computing, object-oriented programming) An area of memory or storage reserved for a particular value, subject to virtual access controls.

(physics) A physical phenomenon (such as force, potential or fluid velocity) that pervades a region; a mathematical model of such a phenomenon that associates each point and time with a scalar, vector or tensor quantity.
A defensive position or effort.
A resolute, unwavering position; firm opinion; action for a purpose in the face of opposition.
A device to hold something upright or aloft.
A small building, booth, or stage, as in a bandstand or hamburger stand.
(intransitive, copulative) To support oneself on the feet in an erect position.
(intransitive, copulative) To maintain one's ground; to be acquitted; not to fail or yield; to be safe.
(intransitive, followed by to + infinitive) To be positioned to gain or lose.
(intransitive) To rise to one’s feet; to stand up.
(transitive) To undergo; withstand; hold up.
(of a statement) Conforming to the actual state of reality or fact; factually correct.

examples.english: It is unfortunately true.
examples.chinese: 可惜这是真的。

current_entries.en_definition: Loyal, faithful.
Genuine; legitimate; valid; sensu stricto.
(logic) Of the state in Boolean logic that indicates an affirmative or positive result.
(of shooting, throwing etc) Accurately; in alignment.
Sound uttered by the mouth, especially by human beings in speech or song; sound thus uttered considered as possessing some special quality or character

examples.english: To my surprise, he had a beautiful voice.
examples.chinese: 他的歌聲出乎意料之外地好聽。

current_entries.en_definition: (grammar) A particular way of inflecting or conjugating verbs, or a particular form of a verb, by means of which is indicated the relation of the subject of the verb to the action which the verb expresses.
(figurative) An expressed opinion, choice, will, desire, or wish; the right or ability to make such expression or to have it considered
(archaic) Command; precept.
(music) In harmony, an independent vocal or instrumental part in a piece of composition.
One who speaks; a speaker.
(transitive) To give utterance or expression to; to utter; to publish; to announce
A definable part, such as an individual song, of a recording, particularly of commercial records, audio tapes, CDs, etc.

examples.english: We are cut from the same cloth.
examples.chinese: 我們很相似。

current_entries.en_definition: (card games) The act or right of dividing a deck of playing cards.
A slab or slice, especially of meat.
An opening of a living body resulting from cutting; an incision or wound.
The manner or style in which a garment, other article of clothing, or sail is fashioned.
To divide with a knife, scissors, or another sharp instrument.
To perform an incision on, for example with a knife.
To abridge or shorten a work; to remove a portion of a recording during editing.
(dated) Firmly or securely fixed in place; stable. [from 9th c.]

examples.english: You're not fast enough.
examples.chinese: 你不够快。

current_entries.en_definition: Moving with great speed, or capable of doing so; swift, rapid. [from 14th c.]
Deep or sound (of sleep); fast asleep (of people). [16th–19th c.]
(dated) Having an extravagant lifestyle or immoral habits. [from 18th c.]
Ahead of the correct time or schedule. [from 19th c.]
In a firm or secure manner, securely; in such a way as not to be moved; safe, sound [from 10th c.].
Quickly, with great speed; within a short time [from 13th c.].
Ahead of the correct time or schedule.
(transitive, sciences) To cause (a person or animal) to abstain, especially from eating.
An occasion of fighting.

examples.english: The argument ended in a fight.
examples.chinese: 讨论最后变成了打架。

current_entries.en_definition: (archaic) A battle between opposing armies.
A physical confrontation or combat between two or more people or groups.
(sports) A boxing or martial arts match.
A conflict, possibly nonphysical, with opposing ideas or forces; strife.
(transitive) To engage in combat with; to oppose physically, to contest with.
(intransitive) To contend in physical conflict, either singly or in war, battle etc.
(transitive) To try to overpower; to fiercely counteract.
(intransitive) To strive for something; to campaign or contend for success.
Strong affection.

examples.english: Love is never wasted.
examples.chinese: 从来就没有徒劳的爱情。

current_entries.en_definition: A person who is the object of romantic feelings; a darling, a sweetheart, a beloved.
(usually, transitive, sometimes, _, intransitive, stative) To have a strong affection for (someone or something).
(usually, transitive, sometimes, _, intransitive) To seek the good or honor of (someone), care deeply about, to be dedicated to (someone or something).
(transitive, euphemistic) To have sex with (perhaps from make love).
(transitive) To be strongly inclined towards something; an emphatic form of like.
(comparable) Receptive.

examples.english: Open your mouth!
examples.chinese: 把嘴张开！

current_entries.en_definition: (not comparable) Actively conducting or prepared to conduct business.
(not comparable) Public.
Not settled; not decided or determined; not withdrawn from consideration.
(mathematics, topology, of a set) Which is part of a predefined collection of subsets of X, that defines a topological space on X.
(sports, and, games) Characterised by free-flowing play.
Of a person, not concealing their feelings, opinions, etc.; candid, ingenuous.
(computing, transitive, intransitive) To connect to a resource (a file, document, etc.) for viewing or editing.
(obsolete) To disclose; to reveal; to interpret; to explain.
A bounding straight edge of a two-dimensional shape.

examples.english: I took it for granted that you were on my side.
examples.chinese: 我理所當然認為你會站在我這邊。

current_entries.en_definition: A region in a specified position with respect to something.
A flat surface of a three-dimensional object; a face.
One half (left or right, top or bottom, front or back, etc.) of something or someone.
One surface of a sheet of paper (used instead of "page", which can mean one or both surfaces.)
One set of competitors in a game.
(law, countable) A product of a legislative body, a statute.

examples.english: You are old enough to know better than to act like that.
examples.chinese: 你夠大了知道不要那樣做。

current_entries.en_definition: The process of doing something.
A formal or official record of something done.
(countable, drama) A division of a theatrical performance.
(obsolete, transitive) To do (something); to perform.
(intransitive) To behave in a certain manner for an indefinite length of time.
(intransitive, law) To carry out work as a legal representative in relation to a particular legal matter.

(intransitive, mathematics, construed with on or upon, of an algebraic structure) To possess an action onto (some other structure). Examples include the group action of a group on a set, the action of a ring on a module by scalar multiplication, and the action of a group or algebra on a vector space via a representation.
A piece of metal placed in a horse's mouth and connected to the reins to direct the animal.

examples.english: With so many people around he naturally became a bit nervous.
examples.chinese: 他被很多人围着，自然变得有点紧张。

current_entries.en_definition: (mathematics, computing) A binary digit, generally represented as a 1 or 0.
(computing) The smallest unit of storage in a digital computer, consisting of a binary digit.
A small amount of something.
(informal) Specifically, a small amount of time.
A portion of something.
The part of a key which enters the lock and acts upon the bolt and tumblers.
(informal) Very good; excellent; wonderful; fantastic. [from 1848]

examples.english: James had a great fear of making mistakes in class and being reprimanded.
examples.chinese: 詹姆斯怕极了在课堂上犯错并被训斥。

current_entries.en_definition: Important, consequential.
(applied to actions, thoughts and feelings) Arising from or possessing idealism; admirable; commanding; illustrious; eminent.
Doing or exemplifying (a characteristic or pursuit) on a large scale; active or enthusiastic.
(informal) Very well (in a very satisfactory manner).
Expression of gladness and content about something.
A sarcastic inversion thereof.
A person of major significance, accomplishment or acclaim.
The surface of the Earth, as opposed to the sky or water or underground.

examples.english: The pirates buried their treasure in the ground.
examples.chinese: 海盗把財寶藏進了地底。

current_entries.en_definition: Soil, earth.
The bottom of a body of water.
Basis, foundation, groundwork, legwork.
(countable, UK) A soccer stadium.
(electricity) An electrical conductor connected to the earth, or a large conductor whose electrical potential is taken as zero (such as a steel chassis).
To found; to fix or set, as on a foundation, reason, or principle; to furnish a ground for; to fix firmly.
An exhibition of items.

examples.english: I was wondering if you were going to show up today.
examples.chinese: 我在想你今天会不会来。

current_entries.en_definition: A broadcast program, especially a light entertainment program.
A demonstration.
(transitive) To display, to have somebody see (something).
(transitive) To indicate (a fact) to be true; to demonstrate.
(intransitive) To be visible; to be seen; to appear.
The total, aggregate or sum of material (not applicable to discrete numbers or units or items in standard English).

examples.english: We will pay you according to the amount of work you do.
examples.chinese: 我們會按照你的工作量付錢給你。

current_entries.en_definition: A quantity or volume.
A large, generally omnivorous mammal (a few species are purely carnivorous or herbivorous), having shaggy fur, a very small tail, and flat feet; a member of the family Ursidae.

examples.english: What?! You ate my chocolate bear?!
examples.chinese: 什么？你把我的巧克力熊吃了？！

current_entries.en_definition: (gay slang) A large, hairy man, especially one who is homosexual. [c. 1970]
(obsolete) A division, a portion, a share, a part, a piece.

examples.english: It's a good deal.
examples.chinese: 这是一笔不错的交易。

current_entries.en_definition: (card games) The distribution of cards to players; a player's turn for this.
A particular instance of trading (buying or selling; exchanging; bartering); a transaction.
An agreement between parties; an arrangement.
(informal) A situation, occasion, or event.
(transitive) To distribute among a number of recipients, to give out as one’s portion or share.
(ambitransitive) To distribute cards to the players in a game.
(of drink or drugs) Strong.

examples.english: You work as hard as he did at your age.
examples.chinese: 你跟他在你這個年紀時一樣努力工作。

current_entries.en_definition: Resistant to pressure; difficult to break, cut, or penetrate.
Severe, harsh, unfriendly, brutal.
Demanding a lot of effort to endure.
Difficult or requiring a lot of effort to do, understand, experience, or deal with.
(manner) With much force or effort.
Lacking (something); without (something). Added usually to a noun to form an adjective signifying a lack of that noun.
To a smaller extent or degree.
A smaller amount of; not as much. [from 12th c.]
(often in the plural) A person who has had a baby; this person in relation to their child or children.

examples.english: He's the new CEO from the parent company in France.
examples.chinese: 他是來自法國母公司的新任執行長。

current_entries.en_definition: The source or origin of something. [from 16th c.]
(biology) An organism from which a plant or animal is immediately biologically descended. [from 17th c.]
(computing) The object from which a child or derived object is descended; a node superior to another node. [from 20th c.]
(computing) The set of all parameters relevant to a computation.

examples.english: Lansing is the state capital of Michigan.
examples.chinese: 蘭辛是密西根州的首府。

current_entries.en_definition: (computing) The stable condition of a processor during a particular clock cycle.
(computing) The values of all parameters at some point in a computation.
(anthropology) A society larger than a tribe. A society large enough to form a state in the sense of a government.
A political division of a federation retaining a notable degree of autonomy, as in the United States, Mexico, Nigeria, or India.
(mathematics, stochastic processes) An element of the range of the random variables that define a random process.
(transitive) To declare to be a fact.
A change of direction or orientation.

examples.english: Turn right at the crossroad.
examples.chinese: 在十字路口右转。

current_entries.en_definition: A chance to use (something) shared in sequence with others.
One's chance to make a move in a game having two or more players.
(intransitive) To change one's direction of travel.
(intransitive, of a body, person, etc) To move about an axis through itself.
(transitive) To change the direction or orientation of, especially by rotation.
(intransitive, or, transitive) To change condition or attitude.
(copulative) To become (begin to be).
(intransitive) To sour or spoil; to go bad.
The game of catching a ball.

examples.english: You have to study hard to catch up with your class.
examples.chinese: 你必須用功讀書趕上同學。

current_entries.en_definition: (countable, cricket, baseball) The act of catching a hit ball before it reaches the ground, resulting in an out.
A stopping mechanism, especially a clasp which stops something from opening.
(transitive) To capture or snare (someone or something which would rather escape). [from 13thc.]
(transitive) To entrap or trip up a person; to deceive. [from 14thc.]
(intransitive) To engage with some mechanism; to stick, to succeed in interacting with something or initiating some process.
(transitive, surfing) To contact a wave in such a way that one can ride it back to shore.
(transitive) To seize or intercept an object moving through the air (or, sometimes, some other medium). [from 16thc.]
(transitive) To grasp mentally: perceive and understand. [from 16thc.]
An inspection or examination.

examples.english: I just wanted to check my email.
examples.chinese: 我只想查一下我的电子邮件。

current_entries.en_definition: (textiles, usually, pluralized) A pattern made up of a grid of squares of alternating colors; a checkered pattern.
A control; a limit or stop.
(chess) A situation in which the king is directly threatened by an opposing piece. [from 14th c.]
(US) A mark (especially a checkmark: ✓) used as an indicator.
(US) A bill, particularly in a restaurant.

(transitive, US, often used with "off") To mark items on a list (with a checkmark or by crossing them out) that have been chosen for keeping or removal or that have been dealt with (for example, completed or verified as correct or satisfactory).
(transitive) To control, limit, or halt.
Transparent in colour.

examples.english: You must clear the table.
examples.chinese: 你必须把桌子清理干净。

current_entries.en_definition: Bright; luminous; not dark or obscured.
Without clouds.
Free of ambiguity or doubt; easily understood.
Free of obstacles.
(intransitive) Of a check or financial transaction, to go through as payment; to be processed so that the money is transferred.
(transitive, intransitive, sports) To hit, kick, head, punch etc. (a ball, puck) away in order to defend one's goal.

Closest or nearest, of a set of futures contracts which expire at particular times, or of the times they expire; (typically, the front month or front year is the next calendar month or year after the current one).

examples.english: All of a sudden, three dogs appeared in front of us.
examples.chinese: 突然之間，三隻狗出現在我們面前。

current_entries.en_definition: The foremost side of something or the end that faces the direction it normally moves.

(meteorology) The interface or transition zone between two airmasses of different density, often resulting in precipitation. Since the temperature distribution is the most important regulator of atmospheric density, a front almost invariably separates airmasses of different temperature.
(military) An area where armies are engaged in conflict, especially the line of contact.
A person or institution acting as the public face of some other, covert group.
An intersection of piping or wiring; an interconnect.

examples.english: It is a pity that you can't join us.
examples.chinese: 你不能和我們一起來真可惜。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To connect or combine into one; to put together.
(intransitive) To come together; to meet.
(transitive) To become a member of.
To come face to face with by accident; to encounter.

examples.english: Everyone wants to meet you. You're famous!
examples.chinese: 大家都想见到你，你太有名了！

current_entries.en_definition: To come face to face with someone by arrangement.
To gather for a formal or social discussion; to hold a meeting.
To perceive; to come to a knowledge of; to have personal acquaintance with; to experience; to suffer.
True, genuine, not merely nominal or apparent.

examples.english: What's your real purpose?
examples.chinese: 你的真正目的是甚麼？

current_entries.en_definition: Genuine, not artificial, counterfeit, or fake.
That has objective, physical existence.
Absolute, complete, utter.
Genuine, unfeigned, sincere.

(mathematics, of a number) Being either a rational number, or the limit of a convergent infinite sequence of rational numbers: being one of a set of numbers with a one-to-one correspondence to the points on a line.
(legal) Relating to immovable tangible property.
Former unit of currency of Spain and Spain's colonies.
A unit of currency used in Brazil since 1994. Symbol: R$.
A commodity; see realty.
The digit/figure 3.

examples.english: You told her that you had finished the work three days before.
examples.chinese: 你告訴她你已經在三天前完成這個工作了。

current_entries.en_definition: A numerical value after two and before four. Represented in Arabic digits as 3; this many dots (•••).
The highest or uppermost part of something.
A framework at the top of a ship's mast to which rigging is attached.
(LGBT slang) A person who penetrates or has a preference for penetrating during intercourse.
Incorrect or untrue.

examples.english: You took the wrong key.
examples.chinese: 你拿错钥匙了。

current_entries.en_definition: Immoral, not good, bad.
Improper; unfit; unsuitable.
(informal) In a way that isn't right; incorrectly, wrongly.
Something that is immoral or not good.
The state of being old; the latter part of life.

examples.english: When I was your age, Pluto was a planet.
examples.chinese: 當我在你這個年紀時，冥王星是行星。

current_entries.en_definition: Any particular stage of life.
Lifespan, lifetime; the total time that some being is alive from birth to death (or some category of beings, on average).

(countable, poker) The entitlement of the player to the left of the dealer to pass the first round in betting, and then to come in last or stay out; also, the player holding this position; the eldest hand.
A particular period of time in history, as distinguished from others.
(astrology) One of the twelve divisions of a Great Year, equal to roughly 2000 years and governed by one of the zodiacal signs; a Platonic month.
(intransitive) To grow aged; to become old or older; to show marks of age.
(transitive) To cause to grow old; to impart the characteristics of age to.
Not here, gone, absent, unavailable, traveling; on vacation.

examples.english: You're just running away from life's problems.
examples.chinese: 你只会逃避生活问题。

current_entries.en_definition: From a place, hence.
At a stated distance in time or space.
With all parts included; with nothing missing; full.
Finished; ended; concluded; completed.
(ambitransitive) To finish; to make done; to reach the end.
Without emotion; impassive.

examples.english: It's a dead end.
examples.chinese: 这是个死胡同。

current_entries.en_definition: (not comparable, sports) Not in play.
(legal) Cut off from the rights of a citizen; deprived of the power of enjoying the rights of property.
(degree, informal, colloquial) Exactly.
synonyms: on the spur of the moment

current_entries.en_definition: (often with "the") Time when coldness, darkness, or stillness is most intense.

(physics) A physical quantity that denotes ability to push, pull, twist or accelerate a body and which has a direction and is measured in a unit dimensioned in mass × distance/time² (ML/T²): SI: newton (N); CGS: dyne (dyn).
Anything that is able to make a substantial change in a person or thing.
(humorous, or, science fiction, with the, often, capitalized) A metaphysical and ubiquitous power from the fictional Star Wars universe created by George Lucas. See usage note. [1977]
The shape or visible structure of a thing or person.

examples.english: You should try to form the habit of using your dictionaries.
examples.chinese: 你们该养成使用字典的习惯。

current_entries.en_definition: A blank document or template to be filled in by the user.
(transitive) To give (a shape or visible structure) to a thing or person.
(transitive) To impose restraint upon; to limit in motion or action; to bind legally or morally; to confine; to restrain.

examples.english: Hold on, someone is knocking at my door.
examples.chinese: 等等，有人在敲我的門。

current_entries.en_definition: To maintain in being or action; to carry on; to prosecute, as a course of conduct or an argument; to continue; to sustain.
(book of the bible) A book of the Old Testament and the Hebrew Tanakh.

examples.english: I like my job very much.
examples.chinese: 我非常喜欢我的工作。

current_entries.en_definition: (biblical character) A character in the Old Testament and the Qur'an, renowned for his patience.
An economic role for which a person is paid.
(archaic) One's inherent nature; character, natural disposition.

examples.english: You must consider what kind of work you want to do.
examples.chinese: 你一定要考慮清楚你想做怎麼樣的工作。

current_entries.en_definition: Goods or services used as payment, as e.g. in barter.
(type theory) The type of a type constructor or a higher-order type operator.
The part of Earth which is not covered by oceans or other bodies of water.

examples.english: Tomorrow, he will land on the moon.
examples.chinese: 明天，他将登上月球。

current_entries.en_definition: Real estate or landed property; a partitioned and measurable area which is owned and acquired and on which buildings and structures can be built and erected.
(agriculture) Any of several portions into which a field is divided for ploughing.
In any surface prepared with indentations, perforations, or grooves, that part of the surface which is not so treated, such as the level part of a millstone between the furrows.
A country or region.
A person's country of origin and/or homeplace; homeland.
(intransitive) To descend to a surface, especially from the air.
(transitive) To bring to land.
(transitive) To acquire; to secure.

(not comparable, euphemistic) Deceased, dead: used particularly when speaking of the dead person's actions while alive. (Generally must be preceded by a possessive or an article, commonly "the"; see usage notes. Can itself only precede the person's name, never follow it.)

examples.english: I was late to school.
examples.chinese: 我上学迟到了。

current_entries.en_definition: After a deadline has passed, past a designated time.
A en given name.

examples.english: What you say is quite wide of the mark.
examples.chinese: 您说的话完全跑题了。

current_entries.en_definition: Mark the Evangelist, also called John Mark, the first patriarch of Alexandria, credited with the authorship of the Gospel of Mark.
(biblical) The Gospel of St. Mark, a book of the New Testament of the Bible. Traditionally the second of the four gospels.
A score for finding the correct answer, or other academic achievement; the sum of such points gained as out of a possible total. [from 19th c.]
A visible impression or sign; a blemish, scratch, or stain, whether accidental or intentional. [from 9th c.]
An indication or sign used for reference or measurement. [from 14th c.]
(historical) A half pound, a former English and Scottish currency equivalent to 13 shillings and fourpence and notionally equivalent to a mark of sterling silver.
(of things) To identify (someone as a particular type of person or as having a particular role).
(Canada, UK) To indicate the correctness of and give a score to (a school assignment, exam answers, etc.).
Healthy.

examples.english: It might sound far-fetched, but this is a real problem.
examples.chinese: 这可能听起来牵强，但却是个真实的问题。

current_entries.en_definition: Complete, solid, or secure.
(British, Irish, slang) Good; acceptable; decent.
(of sleep) Quiet and deep.
A sensation perceived by the ear caused by the vibration of air or some other medium.
A vibration capable of causing such sensations.
(medicine) A long, thin probe for sounding or dilating body cavities or canals such as the urethra; a sonde.
(transitive) To cause to produce a sound.
An attempt.

examples.english: Never try to die.
examples.chinese: 千萬不要自殺。

current_entries.en_definition: An act of tasting or sampling.
(rugby) A score in rugby league and rugby union, analogous to a touchdown in American football.
To prove by experiment; to apply a test to, for the purpose of determining the quality; to examine; to prove; to test.
To settle; to decide; to determine; specifically, to decide by an appeal to arms.
Free from constraints preventing completion of task; permitted to; not prevented from. [First attested from around (1350 to 1470).]

examples.english: Liberty consists of being able to make everything as harmless as possible.
examples.chinese: 自由旨在能够做所有不伤害其他人的事。

current_entries.en_definition: Gifted with skill, intelligence, knowledge, or competence. [First attested in the mid 16th century.]
Close attention; concern; responsibility.

examples.english: I don't care.
examples.chinese: 我无所谓。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive, intransitive) To be concerned (about), to have an interest (in); to feel concern (about).
(intransitive, polite, formal, chiefly in the negative) To want, to desire; to like; to be inclined towards or interested in.
(intransitive) (with for) To look after or look out for.
Sure in one's mind, positive; absolutely confident in the truth of something.

(euphemism, preceded by "a") Used to denote that the speaker is referring to a specific person or thing that they do not want to name directly, implying that the listener should infer the identity of the referent.
Having been determined but not specified.
The top sheet of a bed.

examples.english: Cover your mouth when you cough, sneeze, or yawn.
examples.chinese: 你咳嗽、打喷嚏或打哈欠的时候要捂住你的嘴。

current_entries.en_definition: (music) A new performance or rerecording of a previously recorded song; a cover version; a cover song.
(espionage) A persona maintained by a spy or undercover operative; cover story.
(transitive) To place something over or upon, as to conceal or protect.
(transitive) To be over or upon, as to conceal or protect.

(military, law enforcement, transitive) To protect using an aimed firearm and the threat of firing; or to protect using continuous, heaving fire at or in the direction of the enemy so as to force the enemy to remain in cover; or to threaten using an aimed firearm.
(transitive) To provide an alibi for (someone); to provide excuses or apologia for (someone); to carry water for someone.
(transitive) To copulate with (said of certain male animals such as dogs and horses).
(ambitransitive) To progress through a sequence of stages.

examples.english: Our country must develop its natural resources.
examples.chinese: 我們國家必須開發自然資源。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To advance; to further; to promote the growth of.
For all of a given time interval; throughout; in; pending.

examples.english: I slept a little during lunch break because I was so tired.
examples.chinese: 我在午休时间睡了一会儿，因为我太累了。

current_entries.en_definition: At any time or period within a given time interval.
A vague understanding.

examples.english: For some reason I feel more alive at night.
examples.chinese: 不知道為什麼我夜裡覺得比較有活力。

current_entries.en_definition: (intransitive) To search by sense of touch.
(transitive, copulative) To become aware of through the skin; to use the sense of touch on.
(intransitive, copulative) To experience an emotion or other mental state.
(transitive) To experience an emotion or other mental state about.
(intransitive) To sympathise; to have the sensibilities moved or affected.
(transitive) To think, believe, or have an impression concerning.
(copulative) To seem (through touch or otherwise).
(colloquial) An inexperienced person or one in a junior position.

examples.english: We had a kid just last year.
examples.chinese: 就在去年我們有了一個孩子。

current_entries.en_definition: (informal) A child, adolescent, or (loosely) a young adult.
(intransitive, colloquial) To joke.
Coming afterward in time (used with than when comparing with another time).

examples.english: You'll learn how to do it sooner or later.
examples.chinese: 你遲早會學會怎麼做。

current_entries.en_definition: At some time in the future.
Afterward in time (used with than when comparing with another time).
At some unspecified time in the future.
(Manglish, Singlish) What if (something problematic or unanticipated happens); if not (something undesirable will happen).
The capability for rational thought.

examples.english: I have too many things on my mind these days.
examples.chinese: 这几天我脑袋里的事情太多了。

current_entries.en_definition: The ability to remember things.
The ability to focus the thoughts.
Somebody that embodies certain mental qualities.
Judgment, opinion, or view.
Desire, inclination, or intention.
(frequency) One and only one time.

examples.english: I once wanted to be an astrophysicist.
examples.chinese: 我曾經想過當個太空物理學家。

current_entries.en_definition: (temporal location) Formerly; during some period in the past.
The beginning point of a race, a board game, etc.

examples.english: You'd better start now.
examples.chinese: 你最好現在就開始。

current_entries.en_definition: (intransitive) To have its origin (at), begin.
(ergative, of an object) To come loose, to break free of a firmly set position; to displace or loosen; to dislocate.
(transitive) To disturb and set in motion; to alarm; to rouse; to cause to flee.
A conversation or discussion; usually serious, but informal.

examples.english: I may be antisocial, but it doesn't mean I don't talk to people.
examples.chinese: 我或许不善于社交,但这并不意味我不和别人交谈。

current_entries.en_definition: (preceded by the; often qualified by a following of) A major topic of social discussion.
A lecture.
(uncountable, not preceded by an article) Empty boasting, promises or claims.
(usually, in the plural) Meeting to discuss a particular matter.
(intransitive) To communicate, usually by means of speech.
synonyms: let the cat out of the bag

current_entries.en_definition: A particular time period when guarding is kept.

examples.english: Did you watch the game?
examples.chinese: 你看了比賽嗎？

current_entries.en_definition: A person or group of people who guard.
The post or office of a watchman; also, the place where a watchman is posted, or where a guard is kept.
(transitive) To be wary or cautious of.
(transitive) To attend to dangers to or regarding.
